name: Hyperfoil
description: Hyperfoil — Distributed HTTP load testing framework using async execution for accurate microservice throughput benchmarking.

seo.title: Hyperfoil — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Hyperfoil: Hyperfoil — Distributed HTTP load testing framework using async execution for accurate microservice throughput benchmarking.

when_to_use: Async distributed HTTP load testing for microservices
Accurate throughput benchmarking without client-side bottlenecks
Simulate complex multi-step HTTP scenarios at scale
Measure microservice throughput under sustained load

pros: Distributed agents scale to very high load
Open source from the Red Hat ecosystem
cons: Steep learning curve, YAML scenario model
Smaller community than JMeter or k6
Less tooling around reports and dashboards
review.verdict: Excellent for rigorous microservice latency work, rough around the edges for casual load testing.
review.best_for: Performance engineers who need correct tail latencies at high throughput
review.not_for: Teams that want a gentle k6/JMeter-style learning curve

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free/open-source — no commercial pricing, no HWM.
